title: A resolution expressing support for the designation of the month of April 2025 as "Parkinson's Awareness Month".
plain_summary: This resolution asks Congress to make April 2025 "Parkinson's Awareness Month." Parkinson's disease is a serious illness that affects more than one million Americans and causes problems with movement and other symptoms. The disease is becoming more common and also affects millions of family members who care for people with Parkinson's. The Senate wants to support research to find better treatments and a cure for this disease. They also want to recognize all the people who work to help those living with Parkinson's disease.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions. (text: CR S2719)

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution supports making April two thousand twenty five Parkinson's Awareness Month.

sections.title: Facts About Parkinson's Disease
sections.text: Parkinson's disease affects more than one million people in the United States. Nearly ninety thousand people get diagnosed with it each year. It is the fastest growing brain disease in the world and the second most common type of brain disease that gets worse over time. Scientists think it is caused by a mix of genes and things in the environment, but they still do not know the exact cause for most people. According to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, Parkinson's disease is the fifteenth leading cause of death in the United States. By the year two thousand thirty seven, experts think the number of Americans with Parkinson's disease will almost double. The disease will cost the United States at least eighty billion dollars every year. People with Parkinson's disease can have many different symptoms. These include memory problems and trouble thinking clearly, shaking hands or other body parts, slow movement and stiff muscles, trouble walking and keeping their balance, difficulty speaking and swallowing, depression, and many other problems. Millions of family members, friends, and loved ones have their lives greatly affected when someone they care about has Parkinson's disease. We need more research, education, and community support services to find better treatments and a cure for Parkinson's disease and to help people with the disease keep their dignity.

sections.title: What the Senate Resolves
sections.text: The Senate supports making April two thousand twenty five Parkinson's Awareness Month. The Senate supports the goals and ideas of Parkinson's Awareness Month. The Senate continues to support research to find better treatments and a cure for Parkinson's disease. The Senate recognizes the people living with Parkinson's disease who take part in important medical studies to help us learn more about the disease. The Senate praises the hard work of organizations, volunteers, researchers, and millions of people across the United States who are working to make life better for people living with Parkinson's disease and their families.
